channel.title: Technical Blog on Frank Huang
channel.description: Recent content in Technical Blog on Frank Huang

item.title: What is bitcoin mining?
item.description: Bitcoin has been in the field for a while and friends of mine have been talking about bitcoin for a while. I have also read some of the articles talking about bitcoin, the hashing and the trustful features. But to be honest, these concepts still remain a mist for me. I decided to really figure out them in the way that I think and try to explain it as clear as possible.

item.title: Learning awk and sed
item.description: awk awk '/pattern/ {print $1}' This line of code will print out the first column of the line that contain the pattern in the file. The idea is you want to do something on the line that contain the pattern. Inside the {} is the action you want to do. $columns is which column you want to edit on. awk -F"=" '{print $NF}' inputfile > outputfile sed Some actions: d means delete, p means print, s means substitution.

item.title: TCP connection and disconnection
item.description: TCP建立连接和断开链接一直是个让我很费解的问题，为什么要三番四次的来回折腾呢？于是我决定用形象的说法来讲一下我的理解。 为什么需要三次建立连接 如果采用两次握手的话，打个比方，去饭店吃饭，人太多了要拿号，拿号的动作(SYN_SEND)相当于第一次握手，店家给号的动作(SYN_RECV)相当于第二次握手，如果这样就结束了，那店家作为服务方就会跟你建立连接，也就是保证把位置给你，直到你要结束消费，但是很多情况是我们拿了号之后有可能觉得要等太久了，想换一家吃饭，结果就跑了，这样店家就会一直等我们，导致台位浪费，所以需要额外增加第三次的握手，也就是叫号的动作(ESTABLISHED)。 为什么分手还要四次啊 还是拿饭馆的例子说事，断开连接的过程实际上相当于我们入店消费完成以后要结账的过程。首先我们(client)跟服务员(server)招手，告诉他们我们需要结账(FIN_WAIT1)， 服务员看到后点头示意他们知道了(CLOSE_WAIT)，我们等服务员过来(FIN_WAIT2)，服务员过来以后，再次确认菜都上齐了并且告诉我们最后价格(LAST_ACK)，我们看了一下单子确认价格正确之后付款，然后我们进入(TIME_WAIT)，等待服务员确认收款，最后我们就可以愉快的走人啦。

item.title: Bayesian classifier
item.description: 贝叶斯定理的本质是希望基于已经产生的数据，来预测未来在某一种被认定为原因的概率发生的情况下，再次发生这样的结果的概率是多少。这句话挺起来很绕口，贝叶斯公式美妙的地方就在于能把这样的绕口的表述表现成一个公式。 “’ $$P(A|B)\quad =\quad \frac { P(B|A)\times P(A) }{ P(B) }$$ “’ 如何求解后验概率（P(B|A)）？ 常用方法有MCMC(马尔可夫链蒙特卡洛（Markov chain Monte Carlo)). 而MCMC的实现方法也有很多，常见的有MH. Naive Bayesian classifier 也叫朴素贝叶斯分类器，是贝叶斯分类器的一个特例。在有n个维度的数据集中（假设为x1, x2, … xn），前面所说的P(B|A)实际上等于P(x1, x2,…xn|A)。 当假设每个变量都互相独立的时候即可得P(x1, x2,…xn|A) = P(x1|A) * P(x2|A) … P(xn|A)。这就是朴素贝叶斯定理的条件了。 Reference http://www.cs.ucr.edu/~eamonn/CE/Bayesian%20Classification%20withInsect_examples.pdf https://nlp.stanford.edu/IR-book/html/htmledition/naive-bayes-text-classification-1.html

item.title: Encrypting sensitive information
item.description: I recently have to deal with encrypting some sensitive information which lead me to get to know the encryption algorithms. However, the goal is to encrypt only part of the information in a manner of no decryption. At the mean time, I remember when I was having internet security class back at ESIGELEC during my master degree, my tutor told us that the general way for encryption is using public key and private key which leads to two-way function(decryptable).

item.title: Differences among tuple, list and set in python?
item.description: Basic comparison list: ordered, mutable, duplicable set: set([1, 2, 3, 4]), not duplicable, not ordered # set's operation a = set([1, 2, 3]) b = set([2, 3, 4]) a.intersection(b) # equivalent to c = a & b, return set([2,3]) c.issubset(a) # return true, equivalent to c <= a c.issuperset(a) # return false, equivalent to c >= a a.difference(b) # return set([1]), equivalent to a-b a.symmetric_difference(b) # return set([1,4]), equivalent to a ^ b tuple: immutable, ordered, duplicable

item.title: Dig into PCA -- from my point of view
item.description: This is a topic that I am very interesting about. These concepts are related to a fundamental theory called PCA which is a tool to reduce the dimension of a matrix(dataset) 矩阵对应一个线性变换是在同一组基下有意义。默认情况下是在标准基下进行变换。 $$ \begin{bmatrix} { a }_{ 1,1 } & { a }_{ 1,2 } & \dots & { a }_{ 1,n } \\ \dots & \dots & \dots & \dots \\ { a }_{ m-1,1 } & { a }_{ m-1,2 } & \dots & { a }_{ m-1,n } \\

item.title: Markdown Note
item.description: Building a website is full of fun and a sense of achievement. Markdown has been a popular technique for fullfilling the content of the website due to the efficiency and beauty. So I decided to learn about it and write down my very first blog to record the key point. Making Italic font? blablabla means blablabla Making bold font? blablabla means blablabla Making header? # header means first level header, ## header means second level header etc.

item.title: Migration
item.description: From hexo to django I still love blogging the knowledge that I lean everyday. It is like my RAM is not very large like 2 Gb or so. I really need to flush the things that I read just now to my laptop which performs the function like the hard disk and later on compact them together. From django to hugo Django is great. But for rendering a static webpage like blog or company advertisement, it is better to use a generator framework like hexo though since the process of deploying django is so time consuming.

item.title: Learn pandas
item.description: dataframe.sum() by default, sum up each cell’s value by each column. Specify the axis attribute will to change the sum up rule. 0 means by columns, 1 means by rows. check if the dataframe has null value df.isnull().sum().sum() will sum up all the true value for each column and then each row. replace a certain value with other value df['column name'].map({'yes':1, 'no':0})

item.title: Pom configuration in Maven
item.description: package How to package all the dependencies into one jar <build> <plugins> <plugin> <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId> <artifactId>maven-assembly-plugin</artifactId> <version>2.5.5</version> <configuration> <archive> <manifest> <mainClass>com.xxg.Main</mainClass> </manifest> </archive> <descriptorRefs> <descriptorRef>jar-with-dependencies</descriptorRef> </descriptorRefs> </configuration> <executions> <execution> <id>make-assembly</id> <phase>package</phase> <goals> <goal>single</goal> </goals> </execution> </executions> </plugin> </plugins> </build> package and single equals mvn pakcage assembly:single Reference

item.title: Pom configuration in Maven
item.description: When rendering templates in django, there is one issue that kept me stuck. suppose you have 2 templates in 2 sub-apps. Both of which reference 2 different static files with the same name. This case, django will serve both page with the same static file from the first app(follow the sequence from settings.py installed_apps attribute) I think this would be an issue since it will cause some rendering problems.

item.title: Support vector machine
item.description: v is the point to be classified. w is a vector points to the hyperplane perpendicularly. $$hyperplane:y=w\cdot \xrightarrow { V } +b$$ There are 2 classes which are black and red respectively. if w v + b > 0, then the v belongs to black if w v + b < 0, then the v belongs to red if w * v + v = 0, then the v is on the decision boundary which is also the hyperplane.

item.title: Using bitmap
item.description: Manipulating big data like 1 billion (1 billion == 1,000,000,000 == “十亿”, 1 million == 1,000,000 == “百万” which I always forget how to transform back and forth) , is quite memory consuming if we just use an array for storing it. We can either divide the file into smaller file and process them accordingly (ideas like map reduce, will talk about it later in another post) or use ‘bitmap’ and ‘hashmap’.

item.title: Hbase Design
item.description: HBase is an open source distributed database built on top of hadoop eco-system. It is becoming a popular choice for application who need fast and random access to large amount of data. In this post, I want to explain my summary on reading the paper written by on explaining the design of HBase architecture. This post is not meant to be read at one time since it is too long.
